scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

Ram Kumar,
S/o Shri __________,
Aged about 45 years,
R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – ________.                    ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o Shri __________,
Aged about 38 years,
R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – ________.                    ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The plaintiff above named most respectfully submits as follows:

I. PRELIMINARY SUBMISSIONS

1. The plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is a person of standing and means.

2. The defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is amenable to the jurisdiction of this Court.

3. This Court has pecuniary and territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try this suit as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court and the suit value is within the pecuniary limits of this Court.

II. FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 15.03.2024, the plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the defendant as a hand loan.

5. The said amount of Rs. 15,00,000 was transferred by the plaintiff to the defendant via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456 on 15.03.2024.

6. There was no written agreement between the parties regarding the said loan transaction.

7. The defendant acknowledged the loan through WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties.

8. The defendant failed to repay the loan amount within the agreed time despite repeated oral demands made by the plaintiff.

9. The date of default for repayment was 20.06.2024.

10. The plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be issued to the defendant calling upon the defendant to repay the loan amount.

11. Despite service of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount.

12. The defendant is liable to pay the plaintiff the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 along with interest at 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till realization.

III. STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

13. The statement of account showing the amount due and payable by the defendant to the plaintiff is as under:

(a) Principal amount advanced as hand loan on 15.03.2024: Rs. 15,00,000.00
(b) Part-payments received: Nil
(c) Balance principal due: Rs. 15,00,000.00
(d) Interest @ 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024: As computed
(e) Total amount due: Rs. 15,00,000.00 + Interest

IV. CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the defendant committed default in repayment of the loan amount.

15. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024 when the plaintiff issued a legal notice to the defendant demanding repayment, and the defendant failed to comply with the same.

16. The cause of action is continuing and subsists as the defendant has not repaid the amount due to the plaintiff till date.

V. LIMITATION

17. The suit is within limitation as prescribed under Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years for filing a suit for money lent, and the cause of action accrued on 20.06.2024.

VI. LEGAL BASIS

18. The defendant is bound to repay the loan amount advanced by the plaintiff as per the provisions of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

19. Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides for compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract.

20. This Court has jurisdiction to entertain this civil suit under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which provides that the Court shall have jurisdiction to try all suits of a civil nature excepting suits of which cognizance is either expressly or impliedly barred.

21. This suit is instituted within the local limits of this Court under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the defendant resides and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court.

VII. INTEREST

22. The plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of filing of this suit.

23. The plaintiff further claims interest at such rate as this Court may deem fit and proper under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, on the decretal amount from the date of decree till realization.

VIII. COURT FEE

24. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000 for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction. The plaintiff undertakes to pay the requisite court fee as prescribed under the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, at the time of presentation of this plaint or as directed by this Court.

IX. RELIEFS CLAIMED

25. In view of the facts and circumstances stated above, the plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) against the defendant;
(b) Award interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said amount of Rs. 15,00,000 from 20.06.2024 till the date of decre
